title: Go Pointers
content:
### Pointers in Go

A pointer holds the memory address of a value.

### Declaration

```go
var p *int              // nil pointer to int
i := 42
p = &i                  // p points to i
```

### & and * Operators

| Operator | Meaning | Example |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `&` | Address of | `&i` gives address of i |
| `*` | Dereference | `*p` gives value at p |

### Basic Usage

```go
i := 42
p := &i          // p points to i
fmt.Println(*p)  // 42 (read i through pointer)
*p = 21          // set i through pointer
fmt.Println(i)   // 21
i = 100
fmt.Println(*p)  // 100
```

### Zero Value

The zero value of a pointer is `nil`:

```go
var p *int
fmt.Println(p == nil) // true
// fmt.Println(*p)    // panic: nil pointer dereference
```

### Pointer to Pointer

```go
x := 10
p := &x
pp := &p

fmt.Println(**pp) // 10
```

### Comparing Pointers

Pointers are equal if they point to the same variable:

```go
a, b := 10, 10
pa, pb := &a, &b
fmt.Println(pa == pb) // false (different addresses)

pc := &a
fmt.Println(pa == pc) // true (same address)
```

### Pointers and Structs

```go
type Person struct {
    Name string
    Age  int
}

p := Person{"Alice", 30}
p2 := &p

fmt.Println(p2.Name)  // Alice (no * needed)
p2.Age = 31
fmt.Println(p.Age)    // 31
```

Go automatically dereferences struct pointers for field access (no `->` needed).

> **Key Point:** Unlike C/C++, Go does not allow pointer arithmetic. Pointers are safe and have no dangling reference issues due to garbage collection.
codeExample:
package main

import "fmt"

func main() {
    x := 42
    p := &x

    fmt.Println("value of x:", x)
    fmt.Println("address of x:", p)
    fmt.Println("value through pointer:", *p)

    *p = 100
    fmt.Println("x after *p = 100:", x)

    // Zero value
    var nilPtr *int
    fmt.Println("nil pointer:", nilPtr)
    if nilPtr == nil {
        fmt.Println("nilPtr is nil")
    }
}

quiz.question: What does the & operator do?
options: Returns the address of a variable

quiz.question: What is the zero value of a pointer?
